The Chinese are only capable of making two kinds of products; cheap crap and expensive crap. This is expensive crap. Save some money and buy the cheap crap. Let me explain.

Save money. Go cheap. They all break anyway.

top_profane.text:
What we wanted:
Something that kept stink in, and deterred my son from bringing me a dirty diaper into the living room from his room.

PROS:
- Excellent odor control! We had just a regular trash can before, and were taking the bags outside to the trash on our patio because the smell at the end of one day was just awful. Even when the bag is jammed full of 3 days of dirty diapers, I don't smell a thing.
- There is a 2 point air-tight sealing mechanism on this thing. First you push the soiled diaper through a circle with cut out flaps before it drops into the bag. Then, when you close the diaper pail's lid, a circular spinning mechanism closes around the loose top of the bag, making this thing pretty air-tight.
- Also, as the lid closes, baking soda is dispersed from a little container down on top of your stinky diapers. Awesome thing is that the container is refillable, so you can just buy in bulk and refill yourself, not buy expensive over priced cartridges.
- This appears to be well made. I have only had it about a month, but it seems like something I can save for baby #2 one of these days.
- This comes with 3 of the required bags that are custom built to snap into this thing, and I will say, the refill bags are well built. The plastic is thick, and the top opening is a round circle that snaps into place firmly, so no slipping down insid
top_profane.title: Has it's faults, but does it's job well.

The price doesn't match the junk. The reviews are also questionable. That's what happened to me with this playpen. It's something you'd pay the equivalent of 15-20USD for in one of those made-in-China shops in Europe. That's about what this item is worth. It's yet another thing that comes as a bunch of plastic and weak metal parts shoved in a box, with fabric that not only feels like my 8-month old could rip just by trying to stick her finger through, but also instinctively seems flammable. The fabric is so cheap that it  looks like my baby could roll through it, so I don't know what these reviewers are talking about with older toddlers in this thing. It LOOKS cheap, too. top_rant.text:
****ONE YEAR UPDATE AT THE END****

I did a huge amount of research before jumping into cloth diapering. So, let me share with you what I took away from the research I did, my own personal experience, and some tips specific to these diapers. My tips only relate to this style of diaper - I can give no insight for other types (such as prefolds or all-in-ones as I specifically researched for using this kind).

1. DO YOUR PREP WORK!
The inserts become more absorbing after they get multiple washes – I don’t understand the “why” of this nor do I need to know – but it works! These microfiber inserts need to be washed about 6 (or more if you want) times to get the absorbency up so that they will work well in your diapers. If you buy other inserts (which I recommend, so that you can double up at bedtime or other times – also, there are inserts out there that work better than these, although I haven’t had any issues with these when prepped correctly). If you buy other inserts that have natural material (bamboo, hemp, organic cotton) make certain you wash those by themselves several times to remove any of the natural oils before washing them with your other inserts or diapers! Also, natural fibers need more washes (8+) to be prepped. I didn’t try boiling (no thanks!) like some suggest, so I can’t speak to that.

I put my inserts on the “quick was
top_rant.title: Helpful Tips for Cloth Diapering with These Diapers!  (And a Review too!)

top_rant.text:
My husband and I just purchased the City Mini GT about a month ago and we are both in love with it! Prior to the GT we have been using my sister's Bob 2007 model for about 6 months. At first i was really torn between which one I really preferred. I guess both of these strollers have some amazing features, both have their pluses and minuses. If they could combine these two strollers and make one GOOD one, life would be so much easier! Some of the pros about the City Mini GT vs. the Bob are:
1. The GT is Very compact...definitely not as wide as the Bob, yet it has plenty of room for your little one.
2. The GT has an Awesome large canopy with two windows to see your child. The canopy is very easy to unfold and seems very sturdy as you move it to each position. I have seen the new Bob stroller and although the canopy is also large, it only has one window. Also, when you don't want the canopy expanded on the Bob, the plastic window tends to hang down and is in the way of the child's head...particularly if you have an older child or a tall one. I tried to tuck it in many ways and it always finds its way to hang!
3. The GT has a great reclining back! In its full upright position, my child sits nice and straight. You can recline the back to almost a flat position, which is great if your child falls asleep. It also has a nice mesh behind the child's head for great air
top_rant.title: Awesome stroller!

top_rant.text:
I've been using the BumGenius cloth diapers for 3 months now and I LOVE THEM!! I'll never go back to buying disposable diapers like I did with my first two children and I wish I had discovered these when my first two were in diapers. My first two kids had terrible diaper rashes that we just could not get rid of even after changing diaper and wipe brands and changing diapers frequently. Now with my third baby and using only these cloth diapers with disposable Pampers sensitive wipes, WE HAVEN'T HAD THE FIRST DIAPER RASH! I purchased soft bamboo wash cloths to use as reusable wipes if the sensitive disposable wipes caused rashes, but so far they haven't and I don't mind paying for them.

Honestly, I was a little reluctant to start using cloth diapers and was nervous I'd be grossed out, but it's very easy and I feel so relieved to be rid of the diaper rashes! I'm exclusively breastfeeding my third baby as I did with my first two kids, and the fun fact is that I don't rinse off her diapers before putting them in the wash! I purchased this car seat at the military exchange in Europe - so options were limited to what was on hand, and technically while we could have purchased and returned in new condition within 90 days, there is no real option to try before you buy! With that said, we didn't want to ship anything over to Germany because of the time and cost just to get it here, and the hassle to send it back (and cost) if we didn't like it! So needless to say our experience with the Chicco KeyFit and the other customer reviews plus checking it out in store were the reasons we gave this a try without trying any other seats.

I have a 2012 Volvo XC60 and my husband has an older Range Rover - both have the latch system installed in the outer two rear seats. My daughter is small for her age - she will be 2 years in October and is only 23lbs! She outgrew the KeyFit in length as her head was less than an inch from the top of the seat. I really liked that she could stay rear-facing in this for quite some time given expert guidance that children remain rear-facing as long as possible with up to two years the new recommended minimum. Given her size, I am hoping we get closer to three years!

Ease of Installation and Tight Fit - *****(5/5)
This seat is SO easy to install - took me less than 10 minutes and I actually felt REALLY confident that the fit was right and it was secured.
top_rant.title: Great car seat.
